description_long: This bollard is crafted from a light-colored stone or concrete material, featuring a classical architectural design reminiscent of ancient Greek or Roman columns. The shaft is vertically fluted and tapers gently from a wider base to a narrower top, providing both structural stability and visual elegance. The top of the bollard is adorned with decorative volutes, or spiral scrolls, which are characteristic of the Ionic order in classical architecture. A subtle gold or ochre band encircles the bollard near the base and top, adding a touch of ornamental detail. The base is square and robust, ensuring the bollard remains firmly anchored when installed in urban environments. The overall appearance is both functional and decorative, making it suitable for use in plazas, parks, or as a boundary marker in upscale urban settings.
description: A classical, fluted stone bollard with decorative volutes at the top and subtle gold bands, standing on a square base. Its design is both ornamental and robust, suitable for elegant urban spaces.
description_view_0: Front view showing the fluted shaft and prominent volute scrolls at the top, with gold bands visible near the base and cap.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the smooth, fluted column and the square base, with the volutes less visible from this angle.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, again displaying the fluted shaft and decorative volutes, with gold bands accentuating the design.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the fluted column and square base, with minimal visibility of the volutes.
